---
title: "Experiment Inference Notes"
author: "David Malison"
filters:
  - last-modified.lua
language:
  title-block-published: "Last modified"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
          :root { color-scheme: light dark; }
          main.content {
            max-width: 46rem;
            margin: 0 auto;
            padding: 3rem 1.5rem;
          }
          h1.title {
            font-size: 2.5rem;
            text-align: center;
            margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
          }
          .quarto-title-meta {
            display: flex !important;
            justify-content: center;
            gap: 5rem;
            text-align: center;
          }
          .intro {
            max-width: 40rem;
            margin: 1.75rem auto 0;
            color: #333;
          }
          .notes ul {
            list-style: none;
            padding: 0;
            margin: 2.5rem 0;
          }
          .notes li {
            border: 1px solid #ddd;
            border-radius: 0.5rem;
            padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
            margin: 0.75rem 0;
            transition: background 0.15s ease;
          }
          .notes li:hover { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03); }
          .notes a {
            font-size: 1.15rem;
            font-weight: 600;
            text-decoration: none;
          }
          .notes a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }
          @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .intro { color: #d0d0d0; }
            .notes li { border-color: #333; }
            .notes li:hover { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05); }
          }
        </style>
---

::: {.intro}
These notes develop tools for asymptotic inference in randomized experiments.
We model the data using a fixed-potential-outcomes framework with a small set of transparent assumptions.
The procedures we derive have coverage guarantees that hold uniformly over the parameter space. As a result, they can support reliable inference across a wide range of potential outcome configurations.

:::

::: {.notes}
- [Uniform Inference on Treatment Group Means](treatment-group-means/)
- [Uniform Inference on Relative Treatment Effects](lift/)
- [Regularity Diagnostics](diagnostics/)
:::
